source_quote: FunSearch and DeepSeek-Prover close the loop on a formal/numeric oracle (evaluator or proof kernel) giving a deterministic pass/fail check, which makes RL/MCTS stable.
Aletheia produces natural-language proofs scored by LLM/human judgment; of 200 audited Erdos solutions only 13 (6.5 percent) were rated meaningfully correct.
AI Feynman and symbolic-model discovery fit equations to data with deterministic search and no memory transfer across problems.
ChemCrow and ChemReasoner validate computationally with surrogate signals; physical characterization (NMR, MS) leaves the wet-lab loop open.

GNoME closes a computational loop for phase-stability screening via DFT-in-the-loop active learning, predicting 2.2M structures (380k stable); DFT approximates physical energies and most compounds still require synthesis and characterization (Cheetham and Seshadri 2024).
BioPlanner generates protocols with weak validation signals; biological validity metrics are insufficient to close the loop autonomously.
Automated-social-science and LLM-simulation studies suffer circular validity when the generator and the simulated subjects share a model.
AutoML-Zero, algorithm discovery, and AutoKernel close the loop via reliable benchmark/empirical validation, but the discovered artifacts lack interpretability.
